name: aligned_successor
code:
proof fn phi_1_aligned_successor(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        s > 1,
    ensures
        aligned(a + 1, s),
{
}
reason: Alignment should not be preserved by incrementing the address by 1 for any size > 1

name: aligned_implies_double_size
code:
proof fn phi_2_aligned_implies_double_size(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        s > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(a, 2 * s),
{
}
reason: Alignment to s should not imply alignment to 2*s (e.g., 2 is aligned to 2 but not to 4)

name: aligned_pred
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_pred(s: nat)
    requires
        s > 1,
    ensures
        aligned((s - 1) as nat, s),
{
}
reason: The value s-1 should never be aligned to s when s > 1 (e.g., 1 is not aligned to 2)

name: aligned_sum_off_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_sum_off_by_one(a: nat, b: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        aligned(b, s),
        s > 1,
    ensures
        aligned(a + b + 1, s),
{
}
reason: Sum of two aligned values plus 1 should not be aligned for size > 1, since alignment is closed under addition but not under +1

name: aligned_product_distrib
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_product_distrib(a: nat, b: nat, s: nat, t: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        aligned(b, t),
        s > 0,
        t > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(a + b, s * t),
{
}
reason: Alignment to different moduli should not distribute over addition into their product (e.g., aligned(2,2) and aligned(3,3) but 5 is not aligned to 6)
